summary:
A marathon runner experienced persistent anterior-medial tibia pain while jogging; an x-ray was inconclusive but a bone scan could reveal underlying issues such as shin splints or minor stress injuries consistent with overuse. Given these details, the likely diagnosis remains medial tibial stress syndrome ('shin splints'). To confirm this suspicion, further diagnostic imaging via a bone scan will provide necessary insights into any potential soft tissue damage or initial stages of osseous pathology unapparent by standard radiographs. ```plaintext
A marathon runner has persisting anterior-medial tibia pain during extended activities, which suggests possible medial tibial stress syndrome ('shin splints') despite negative X-rays. Confirmatory evidence can come from a bone scan indicating small-scale structural changes indicative of repetitive strain injury rather than more severe fractures.
```
